nodes.name: Webhook — Receive Podcast Payload
nodes.notes:
ENTRY POINT — Receives POST from client OpenClaw/Skynet instance.
U7 (2026-07-16): SYNCHRONOUS response (responseMode=responseNode). The caller's HTTP connection is held open until a Respond to Webhook node fires on whichever branch this execution takes (entry-guard refusal 422, standing/identity refusal 403, publish success 200, publish failure 500). No longer fire-and-forget.

REQUIRED FIELDS IN BODY:
- podcast_id      : Podbean channel identifier
- audio_url       : GHL Media Library URL for episode MP3
- image_url       : GHL Media Library URL for cover art (JPEG or PNG only, square 1:1, min 1400x1400px)
- title           : Episode title
- description     : Show notes (plain text or HTML)
- publish_date    : ISO format e.g. 2025-01-28T09:00:00
- client_email    : Where to send the success or failure notification

OPTIONAL FIELDS (defaults applied if omitted):
- episode_type    : full | trailer | bonus  (default: full)
- explicit        : clean | explicit        (default: clean)

IMAGE FORMAT RULES:
JPEG (.jpg) and PNG (.png) are the only accepted formats.
WebP, GIF, and all other formats will be rejected at the Prepare Image Upload node
before any Podbean API calls are made.

nodes.name: Compute Timestamp — Convert Publish Date to Unix
nodes.notes:
Converts ISO publish_date from webhook to Unix epoch seconds.
Podbean requires Unix timestamp for scheduled publishing via publish_timestamp field.
Also produces a formatted EST date string used in the client notification email.

nodes.name: Podbean OAuth — Get Access Token
nodes.notes:
Authenticates with Podbean API using BlackCEO platform credentials.
The podcast_id parameter scopes this access token to the specific client channel.
Token is short-lived and required for all subsequent Podbean API calls in this workflow.
Credentials are held here — clients never see the client_id or client_secret.

nodes.name: Podbean — Fetch Recent Episodes
nodes.notes:
Fetches the 10 most recent episodes from the client Podbean channel.
Used to determine the highest existing episode number for sequential numbering.
Fetches 10 rather than 1 to account for episodes that may have been published out of chronological order.
Eliminates the need for a separate tracking table in n8n.

nodes.name: Compute Next Episode Number — Increment From Last
nodes.notes:
Scans fetched episodes for the highest episode_number and adds 1.
Fallback: uses total episode count + 1 if no episode numbers are set.
Edge case: starts at 1 for brand new channels with no episodes.
Pulls directly from Podbean so the count is always accurate — no drift from manual publishes.

assignments.name: publish_timestamp
assignments.value: ={{ $('Compute Timestamp — Convert Publish Date to Unix').item.json.publish_timestamp }}

assignments.name: publish_date_readable
assignments.value: ={{ $('Compute Timestamp — Convert Publish Date to Unix').item.json.publish_date_readable }}

assignments.name: episode_number
assignments.value: ={{ $('Compute Next Episode Number — Increment From Last').item.json.next_episode_number }}

nodes.name: Set Config — Consolidate Workflow Variables
nodes.notes:
SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for all variables used throughout this workflow.
Pulls from: webhook payload, computed timestamp, episode number calculation, and OAuth token.
All downstream nodes reference this node for metadata rather than reaching back to individual upstream nodes.
Defaults applied here: episode_type defaults to 'full', explicit defaults to 'clean'.

nodes.name: Download Audio — Fetch From GHL URL
nodes.notes:
Downloads the episode audio file as binary from the GHL Media Library URL.
GHL Media Library URLs are publicly accessible without auth headers — reliable for direct binary download.
Output is stored as binary property 'audio' for use in the Podbean S3 upload chain.

nodes.name: Prepare Audio Upload — Package for Podbean S3
nodes.notes:
Packages audio binary for S3 upload.
Calculates byte filesize — Podbean requires this to pre-allocate S3 storage in the uploadAuthorize step.
Builds zero-padded filename (episode_0001.mp3) for consistent channel organization.
Passes binary through explicitly to prevent data loss when flowing into the next JSON node.

nodes.name: Podbean — uploadAuthorize Audio
nodes.notes:
Requests a presigned S3 upload URL from Podbean for the audio file.
Podbean pre-allocates S3 storage using the filename and filesize provided.
Returns:
  presigned_url — the S3 URL where we PUT the file
  file_key      — Podbean's internal S3 identifier, becomes media_key in the publish call

nodes.name: Merge Audio Binary — Reattach to Presigned URL
nodes.notes:
n8n HTTP Request nodes drop binary data when processing JSON responses.
Retrieves audio binary from Prepare Audio Upload and reunites it with the presigned URL from uploadAuthorize.
Without this merge the PUT node would have a target URL but no file content to upload.

nodes.name: PUT Audio — Upload to Podbean S3
nodes.notes:
Uploads episode MP3 binary directly to Podbean S3 via the presigned URL.
This is a direct S3 PUT — authentication is encoded in the presigned URL itself, no additional auth headers needed.
A successful 200 or 204 response means the file_key from uploadAuthorize is now a valid media_key for publishing.

nodes.name: Download Image — Fetch From GHL URL
nodes.notes:
Downloads the cover art binary from the GHL Media Library URL.
Podbean cannot accept external image URLs for episode artwork — the image must be uploaded to Podbean's own S3 and referenced by a logo_key.
This download initiates that upload chain.

nodes.name: Prepare Image Upload — Package for Podbean S3
nodes.notes:
Packages cover image binary for Podbean S3 upload.
Detects image format from the binary mimeType — dynamically handles JPEG and PNG.
REJECTS WebP, GIF, and all other formats with a clear error message — Apple Podcasts only accepts JPEG and PNG and Podbean enforces this downstream.
Calculates byte filesize for Podbean uploadAuthorize pre-allocation.
Captures audio file_key from uploadAuthorize Audio and carries it forward as media_key to keep Set Upload Keys clean.

nodes.name: Podbean — uploadAuthorize Image
nodes.notes:
Requests a presigned S3 URL from Podbean for the cover image upload.
Same pattern as uploadAuthorize Audio.
Returns:
  presigned_url — the S3 URL where we PUT the image
  file_key      — Podbean's internal S3 identifier, becomes logo_key in the publish call

nodes.name: Merge Image Binary — Reattach to Presigned URL
nodes.notes:
Retrieves image binary from Prepare Image Upload and reattaches it to the presigned URL from uploadAuthorize Image.
Identical pattern to the audio merge — required because binary data does not survive HTTP Request JSON response nodes.
Without this merge the PUT node has a target URL but no image content.

parameters.name: Content-Type
parameters.value: ={{ $('Prepare Image Upload — Package for Podbean S3').item.json.imageContentType }}

nodes.name: PUT Image — Upload to Podbean S3
nodes.notes:
Uploads cover image to Podbean S3 via presigned URL.
Content-Type header is dynamic — set from the detected format in Prepare Image Upload (image/jpeg or image/png).
After successful upload, the file_key from uploadAuthorize Image is a valid logo_key for the episode publish call.

assignments.name: media_key
assignments.value: ={{ $('Prepare Image Upload — Package for Podbean S3').item.json.media_key }}

nodes.name: Set Upload Keys — Assemble Publish Payload
nodes.notes:
Consolidates everything needed for the Podbean publish API call into one clean object.
media_key = Podbean S3 identifier for the uploaded audio file (from uploadAuthorize Audio)
logo_key  = Podbean S3 identifier for the uploaded cover image (from uploadAuthorize Image)
Both keys are only available AFTER their respective file uploads complete, which is why this Set node exists here rather than in Set Config.

nodes.name: IF — Episode Created Successfully
nodes.notes:
Checks for a valid episode ID in the Podbean response. Works for BOTH published episodes AND scheduled drafts.
Output 0 (True)  — episode ID present: create succeeded.
Output 1 (False) — no episode ID: create failed.

parameters.subject: ={{ '\u274C Podcast Publish Failed — Episode #' + $('Set Upload Keys — Assemble Publish Payload').item.json.episode_number }}
parameters.message: (HTML notification template — reads all fields via expressions off 'Set Upload Keys — Assemble Publish Payload' and the Podbean response; contains no literal client data)

nodes.name: Gmail — Failure Notification
nodes.notes: Sends failure notification when Podbean returns no valid episode ID.

nodes.name: Podbean — Publish Episode
nodes.notes:
STATUS LOGIC (U9 fix 2026-07-16 — was 'draft', which Podbean's own API doc + Help Center confirm does NOT auto-publish; a scheduled episode must use 'future'):
Future timestamp: status='future' (Podbean auto-publishes at publish_timestamp)
Past/now timestamp: status='publish' (immediate release)
Parity with repo script 58-podcast-production-engine/scripts/podbean_publish.sh (lines 419-431), which already used 'future' correctly.

parameters.subject: ={{ $json.episode?.permalink_url ? '\u2705 Episode #' + $('Set Upload Keys — Assemble Publish Payload').item.json.episode_number + ' Is Live — ' + $('Set Upload Keys — Assemble Publish Payload').item.json.title : '\u2705 Episode #' + $('Set Upload Keys — Assemble Publish Payload').item.json.episode_number + ' Scheduled — ' + $('Set Upload Keys — Assemble Publish Payload').item.json.title }}
parameters.message: (HTML notification template — reads all fields via expressions off 'Set Upload Keys — Assemble Publish Payload' and the Podbean response; contains no literal client data)

parameters.subject: ={{ 'Podcast Publish Refused at Entry Guard - ' + $json.title }}
parameters.message: (HTML notification template — reads all fields via expressions off the entry-guard verdict; contains no literal client data)

nodes.name: Standing Gate — Roster Lookup By Email
Gmail — Standing Identity Gate Refused Notification

parameters.subject: ={{ 'Podcast Publish Refused at Standing/Identity Gate - ' + $json.standing_gate_verdict }}
parameters.message: (HTML notification template — reads all fields via expressions off the standing-gate verdict; contains no literal client data)

parameters.responseBody: ={{ { ok: false, reason: 'invalid_payload', missing: $json.guard_missing_fields, bad_urls: $json.guard_bad_url_fields, message: $json.guard_error } }}
={{ { ok: false, reason: $json.standing_gate_verdict, message: $json.standing_gate_verdict === 'not_in_good_standing' ? 'you are not in good standing' : undefined } }}

={{ { ok: true, permalink_url: $json.episode?.permalink_url || '', episode_id: $json.episode?.id || $json.id || '', episode_number: $('Set Upload Keys — Assemble Publish Payload').item.json.episode_number, scheduled: $('Set Upload Keys — Assemble Publish Payload').item.json.publish_timestamp > Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000), idempotent_replay: false } }}

nodes.name: Idempotency — Lookup By Key
nodes.notes: U8 — SERVER-SIDE IDEMPOTENCY. Placed after the U6 entry guard, before the U5 standing/identity gate (spec Section 5 U8). Looks up podcast_publish_ledger by idempotency_key. onError=continueRegularOutput + alwaysOutputData=true (same fix required by U5/U13) so a no-match or table error always yields exactly one output item, never a silently-halted execution.

nodes.name: Media Preflight — HEAD Check Audio URL

parameters.url: ={{ ($('Webhook — Receive Podcast Payload').first().json.body || $('Webhook — Receive Podcast Payload').first().json).audio_url }}

nodes.notes: U11 — MEDIA PREFLIGHT (spec Section 5 U11). HEAD-checks audio_url before ANY Podbean call (extends the GK-01 principle: refuse before ANY Podbean byte moves). fullResponse:true + neverError:true so a 4xx/5xx surfaces as a normal item (statusCode inspected downstream) instead of throwing; onError=continueRegularOutput covers genuine connection-level failures (DNS/timeout/refused) the same way, matching the alwaysOutputData pattern U5/U8/U13 already established for this workflow. Reads the URL from the raw webhook payload, same source Set Config — Consolidate Workflow Variables uses.

nodes.name: Media Preflight — HEAD Check Image URL

parameters.url: ={{ ($('Webhook — Receive Podcast Payload').first().json.body || $('Webhook — Receive Podcast Payload').first().json).image_url }}

nodes.notes: U11 — MEDIA PREFLIGHT (spec Section 5 U11). HEAD-checks image_url before ANY Podbean call. Same tolerant-of-errors pattern as the audio check; runs sequentially after it so the verdict node can report BOTH failing URLs together, not just the first.

parameters.subject: ={{ 'Podcast Publish Refused at Media Preflight - ' + $json.media_preflight_failing_urls.join(', ') }}
parameters.message: (HTML notification template — reads all fields via expressions off the media-preflight verdict; contains no literal client data)

main.node: Compute Next Episode Number — Increment From Last
Download Audio — Fetch From GHL URL
Prepare Audio Upload — Package for Podbean S3
Merge Audio Binary — Reattach to Presigned URL
PUT Audio — Upload to Podbean S3
Download Image — Fetch From GHL URL
Prepare Image Upload — Package for Podbean S3
Merge Image Binary — Reattach to Presigned URL
PUT Image — Upload to Podbean S3
Set Upload Keys — Assemble Publish Payload
Standing Gate — Roster Lookup By Email
Media Preflight — HEAD Check Audio URL
Gmail — Standing Identity Gate Refused Notification
Media Preflight — HEAD Check Image URL
Compute Timestamp — Convert Publish Date to Unix
